channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [commentary].
item.description: A commentary published in the Journal of Physiotherapy discusses that adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology evaluated the measurement properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke, finding that while widely used, the quality and certainty of evidence regarding its psychometric properties have been unclear.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial with 60 patients undergoing proximal hamstring tendon repair for acute avulsions found that a conservative rehabilitation (CR) pathway and an accelerated rehabilitation (AR) pathway yielded comparable 2-year clinical outcomes, strength recovery, functional symmetry, and complication/re-injury rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [synopsis].
item.description: A critically appraised paper indicates that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains (functioning, general well-being, and social/environmental), to standardize assessment in practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: This RCT found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists (APP role) and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for non-urgent musculoskeletal conditions in the emergency department.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients undergoing elective ACDF found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration development within 3 years of index surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Role of Platelet-Rich Plasma Injection in Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
item.description: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials investigated the role of platelet-rich plasma injection in anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women - European Journal of Applied Physiology
item.description: This research found that personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improved multisystem function in overweight and obese older women.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT involving 30 chronic stroke patients found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device improved flexor spasticity and hand function, offering a potential tool for long-term management.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Understanding Adult Hip Dysplasia: Clinical Presentation, Assessment Techniques, and Exercise-Driven Management Strategies
item.description: This review provides an overview of adult hip dysplasia, focusing on its clinical presentation, assessment techniques, and exercise-driven management strategies to improve recognition and intervention.
item.author: JOSPT

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease, comparing rTMS with sham stimulation and investigating modulating factors.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review established weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury to address high recurrence rates, analyzing studies from PubMed, Sportdiscuss, WOS, and Scielo databases.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review with meta-analysis of 17 trials (1,372 participants) found shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.15, -0.29]) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Predictive socio-cultural factors of pain intensity, disability, and quality of life in patients with nonspecific musculoskeletal pain: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: A prospective cohort study of 1350 Dutch primary care physiotherapy patients found that socio-cultural factors predict short- and long-term outcomes of nonspecific musculoskeletal pain (NSMSP), and are associated with baseline and follow-up pain intensity, disability, and health-related quality of life.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Reliability of digital algometer-based pressure pain threshold measurement in patients with end-stage knee osteoarthritis: a single-center reliability study.
item.description: A prospective observational reliability study with 60 end-stage knee osteoarthritis patients found excellent intra-rater and inter-rater reliability for digital algometer-based pressure pain threshold assessment (ICC > 0.90), both at local and remote sites.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mindset, Effort, and Support-the foundation for patients' decision to return to sport after an anterior cruciate ligament injury. A qualitative analysis from the NACOX Cohort.
item.description: A qualitative analysis of 112 patients from the NACOX Cohort with acute ACL injury explored factors determining return to sport (RTS) decisions, categorizing 271 meaning units into "motivation and driving forces," "treatment and recovery," and "medical clearance and readiness."
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy of an Intensive Community-Based Next-Generation NeuroAnimation Therapy in Reducing Upper Extremity Impairment after Stroke: Small Retrospective Cohort Study
item.description: A retrospective pre-test post-test study of a cohort of community-dwelling stroke survivors participating in a high-dose, high-intensity Next-Generation NeuroAnimation Therapy (NG-NAT) found that the intervention was associated with reduced upper extremity motor impairment.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Consensus practice guidelines on interventions for cervical spine (facet) joint pain from a multispecialty international working group
item.description: Consensus practice guidelines on interventions for cervical spine (facet) joint pain were developed by a multispecialty international working group.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Use of Extracorporeal Shockwave Therapy for the Management of Bone Pathologies: A Systematic Review
item.description: A systematic review evaluated the efficacy and outcomes of extracorporeal shockwave therapy (ESWT) for bone pathologies, including fractures, osteonecrosis/avascular necrosis, bone stress injury, medial tibial stress syndrome, and bone marrow edema syndrome.
item.author: PubMed
